title: Industrial Policy and Technology
gist: A MERICS/ETNC topic page framing Europe as fragmented and belatedly awakening to China as a technology and innovation power, with Germany's stance shifting from mutual benefit to existential competition and rising focus on Chinese semiconductor advances.

themes.theme: Europe's fragmented response to China's tech rise

insights.claim: A European Think-tank Network on China (ETNC) report frames Europe as 'fragmented' in dealing with China as a technology and innovation power.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Fragmentation implies slow, inconsistent EU tech policy toward China, a structural weakness benefiting Chinese market access.
insights.quote: Fragmented Europe: Dealing with China as a technology and innovation power

insights.claim: MERICS characterizes the EU response as Europe 'awakening' to Chinese technology only when 'shepherded by Brussels.'
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Suggests centralized Brussels-led coordination, not member states, is the driver of any coherent EU tech-China policy.
insights.quote: Shepherded by Brussels, Europe awakens to Chinese technology

insights.claim: MERICS frames Germany's relationship with China as moving 'from mutual benefit to existential competition.'
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Signals a structural hardening of Germany's China stance, with implications for German industrial and export exposure.
insights.quote: Germany: From mutual benefit to existential competition with China

themes.theme: China's semiconductor advances on Europe's radar

insights.claim: MERICS convened a closed-door workshop on China's advancements in new semiconductor technologies and their implications for Europe.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Indicates European policy circles are actively tracking Chinese semiconductor progress as an emerging strategic concern.
insights.quote: China’s Advancements in New Semiconductor Technologies and Their Implications for Europe
